response:
TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY ADDING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THIS MEANS THE RADIUS OF EACH CIRCLE IS MULTIPLIED BY 1.1 TO GET THE NEXT RADIUS. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS GIVEN BY THE FORMULA πR². BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF ALL 8 CIRCLES, THE TOTAL AREA CAN BE EXPRESSED AS A FUNCTION OF R AND π. THE FINAL EXPRESSION IS πR²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + (1.1)⁶ + (1.1)⁸ + (1.1)¹⁰ + (1.1)¹² + (1.1)¹⁴). THIS FORMULA ACCOUNTS FOR THE GROWING RADIUS AND PROVIDES THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY THE CIRCLES.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS USED TO PLACE TEXT. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS FOUND USING THE FORMULA 2πR, WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS R*(1.1)^4. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS THEN 2πR*(1.1)^4. TO DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE, THE TOTAL LENGTH IS DIVIDED BY THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER, WHICH IS 0.5 CM. THE RESULT IS THEN CHECKED TO ENSURE IT IS AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE INTEGER PART OF THE DIVISION. THIS PROCESS ENSURES THE TEXT IS EVENLY SPACED AND MEETS THE DESIGN REQUIREMENTS.
